dana-lane.position: centered in all three sub-panels, seen from behind at a low angle, torso filling the frame from hips to shoulders, head turned slightly right so the edge of her jaw is visible past her hair

pose.overall: standing, weight even, back square to camera in all three sub-panels — identical framing, only the musculature and fabric damage change stage to stage
pose.left_arm: at her side, slightly flexed; fist clenching progressively harder each stage
pose.right_arm: at her side, mirroring the left; by stage 3 both forearms flare outward as the lats widen
pose.hands: exactly two hands, both her own, both visible at her sides as clenched fists — they touch nothing

dana-lane.expression: face mostly hidden from behind; the visible sliver of jaw and cheek reads gritted-teeth strain in stage 2 and an exhilarated half-smile by stage 3

spatial_rules: exactly one person in the image
exactly two hands total: her left fist and right fist at her sides
three equal vertical sub-panels, same camera, same distance, same lighting — time passes left to right

stage 1: blouse back taut, shoulder blades defined; stage 2: lats flaring wide, the back seam splitting downward; stage 3: full muscular cobra back exposed through the split, trapezius mountains, the blouse hanging on only as sleeves — growth strictly monotonic, never reverting
lighting: harsh overhead laboratory floods raking down the back, cool concrete bounce — identical across all three sub-panels
